channel.title: Robotic on Abdulkadir Güngör
channel.description: Recent content in Robotic on Abdulkadir Güngör

item.title: Nur-o-link: Remote-Controlled Robotic Arm and Vehicle System
item.description:
In the world of robotics, control and mobility are the two most critical factors that determine the functionality of systems. The Nur-o-link project offers a flexible and versatile robotic platform by combining both a precise robotic arm mechanism and a remote-controllable vehicle system.

1. Architectural Approach: Hybrid Robotic Design

Nur-o-link is not just a tool, but a manipulator system capable of performing tasks in the physical world. The project successfully integrates the vehicle’s mobility with the gripping and carrying capabilities of a robotic arm on the same embedded system.

item.title: Gungor-robot-car: ESP32 Camera-Controlled Robot Car
item.description:
Gungor-robot-car is a robotic vehicle project designed using the ESP32-WROVER module and a camera module, providing live video monitoring and remote movement control via a browser.

1. Architectural Approach

The project acts as an HTTP server using the ESP32’s internal WiFi capabilities. Users can manage the vehicle and view the live video stream (MJPEG) by connecting to a browser-based interface over the local network.

item.title: The Evolution of Robotic Systems and Modern Migration Strategies to the ROS 2 Ecosystem
item.description: The world of robotics has undergone a massive evolution over the last decade, shifting from monolithic structures to distributed and modular architectures. Robot Operating System (ROS), which sits at the center of this evolution—particularly with versions like “Lush” and “Noetic”—has set academic and industrial standards. However, modern needs such as real-time data processing, industrial safety standards, and multi-robot coordination have begun to push the limits of the original ROS architecture. At this point, ROS 2, with its new architecture built upon the Data Distribution Service (DDS) layer, is moving robotic systems out of the laboratory environment and into critical industrial operational fields.
